window_summary: This window tracks the recent Textualize/textual release stream from v6.6.0 through v8.2.7, covering widget enhancements, performance optimizations, new themes, and breaking changes like the BLANK to NULL rename.

chunks.title: The mark of the Best release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The mark of the Best release
Tag: v6.6.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-11-10T17:54:38Z; created_at: 2025-11-10T17:54:09Z
Release notes:
A few minor updates and fixes.
Also a style change for the checkbox widget. Expect snapshot test files if you have used checkboxes.
Thanks to the contributors!
## [6.6.0] - 2025-11-10
### Fixed
- Fixed `TextArea` cursor display on wrapped lines https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6196
- Fixed `remove_children` not refreshing layout https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6206
- Fixed flicker with :hover pseudo class https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6214
- Fixed scrollbar not updating after textarea paste https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6219
### Added
- Added `grid_size` property to `GridLayout` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6210
- Exposed `NoSelection` and `BLANK` via `textual.widgets.select` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6214
- Added `Widget.FOCUS_ON_CLICK` classvar amd `Widget.focus_on_click` method https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6216
- Added support for the kitty keyboard protocol on Windows https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6207
- Added `Widget.mount_compose` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6216
### Changed
- Change highlight style of Select to only highlight the border, not the label https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6214
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v6.6.0, the project delivers minor updates and fixes, including a style change for the checkbox widget that may affect snapshot tests. Fixes address TextArea cursor display on wrapped lines, remove_children layout refresh, hover pseudo-class flicker, and scrollbar update after textarea paste. Additions include a grid_size property for GridLayout, exposure of NoSelection and BLANK via textual.widgets.select, Widget.FOCUS_ON_CLICK classvar and focus_on_click method, support for the kitty keyboard protocol on Windows, and Widget.mount_compose. The highlight style of Select is changed to only highlight the border, not the label.

chunks.title: The 6.7 - if you know, you know release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The 6.7 - if you know, you know release
Tag: v6.7.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-11-29T16:34:48Z; created_at: 2025-11-29T16:30:48Z
Release notes:
## [6.7.0] - 2025-11-29
### Added
- Added `GridLayout.max_column_width` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6228
- Added `Content.fold` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6238
- Added `strip_control_codes` to Content constructors https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6238
### Changed
- Added `Screen.get_loading_widget` which deferes to `App.get_loading_widget` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6228
### Fixed
- Fixed `anchor` with `ScrollView` widgets https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6228
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v6.7.0, new features include GridLayout.max_column_width, Content.fold, and strip_control_codes in Content constructors. Screen.get_loading_widget is added, deferring to App.get_loading_widget. A fix addresses anchor with ScrollView widgets.

chunks.title: The grown up Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The grown up Release
Tag: v6.8.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-12-07T17:55:46Z; created_at: 2025-12-07T17:52:59Z
Release notes:
A few fixes, and a very minor feature...
## [6.8.0] - 2025-12-07
### Added
- Added `Content.blank` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6264
### Fixed
- Fixed `Input` cursor color display in ANSI mode (`ansi_color=True`) https://github.com/Textualize/textual/issues/6234
- Fixed alt modifier on systems without extended Key Protocol https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6267
- Fixed an issue where alpha keys with modifiers weren't lower cased. If you have bound to something like `ctrl+A`, then change to `ctrl+shift+a` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6267
- Fixed exception when setting `loading` attribute before mount https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6268
- Fixed issue with dim filter not using background (may cause snapshot failures) https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6269
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v6.8.0, Content.blank is added. Fixes include Input cursor color display in ANSI mode, alt modifier handling on systems without extended Key Protocol, lowercasing of alpha keys with modifiers, exception when setting loading attribute before mount, and dim filter background issue that may cause snapshot failures.

chunks.title: The Smol Paws release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Smol Paws release
Tag: v6.12.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-02T09:45:06Z; created_at: 2026-01-02T09:41:40Z
Release notes:
A small update to address a performance issue. Previously if you dismiss a screen and the base screen has a lot of widgets, you would could get a noticeable pause (anything up to half a second). With a reasonable number of widgets you would probably not notice. But this update fixes that.
## [6.12.0] - 2025-01-02
### Fixed
- Fixed unnecessary style update when popping screens, which may have caused noticable pauses changing screens (with a lot of widgets) https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6304
### Changed
- Promoted private `_update_styes` to `update_node_styles` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6304
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v6.12.0, a performance issue is fixed where unnecessary style updates when popping screens caused noticeable pauses, especially with many widgets. The private _update_styes is promoted to update_node_styles.

chunks.title: The Atomic Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Atomic Release
Tag: v7.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-03T11:51:19Z; created_at: 2026-01-03T11:35:08Z
Release notes:
This is a much smaller change than the version number may suggest. A breaking change to a method added just a few days ago. But Semver mandates the major version bump.
This release has two new themes, thanks to @NSPC911
## [7.0.0] - 2026-01-03
### Changed
- `Node.update_node_styles` has grown a `animate` parameter
### Added
- Adde atom-one-dark and atom-one-light themes @NSPC911 https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6301
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v7.0.0, a breaking change modifies Node.update_node_styles to include an animate parameter. Two new themes, atom-one-dark and atom-one-light, are added.

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v7.0.1, a refresh_styles boolean is added to the ScreenResult message to reduce style updates when popping screens, speeding up transitions.

chunks.title: The BLANK Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The BLANK Release
Tag: v7.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-10T10:33:18Z; created_at: 2026-01-10T10:31:32Z
Release notes:
## [7.1.0] - 2026-01-10
### Fixed
- Fixed issue with missing refresh https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6318
### Added
- Added Widget.BLANK which can optimize rendering of large widgets (typically containers that scroll) https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6318
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v7.1.0, a missing refresh issue is fixed, and Widget.BLANK is added to optimize rendering of large widgets, typically scrolling containers.

chunks.title: The Helping Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Helping Release
Tag: v7.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-11T17:43:00Z; created_at: 2026-01-11T17:39:58Z
Release notes:
Small update to the help system. The help panel will look for a HELP attribute on the focused widget, but now it will also look at ancestors until it finds a usable HELP attribute. There are also a few changes to add more titles to the keys area.
## [7.2.0] - 2026-01-11
### Changed
- The help panel will look at ancestor widgets for a `HELP` attribute if there isn't one on the focused widget https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6320
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v7.2.0, the help panel is changed to look at ancestor widgets for a HELP attribute if the focused widget lacks one, and more titles are added to the keys area.

chunks.title: The Super release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Super release
Tag: v7.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-15T16:33:18Z; created_at: 2026-01-15T16:31:11Z
Release notes:
A few fixes and small features. Enjoy.
## [7.3.0] - 2026-01-15
### Fixed
- Fixed triple click on command palette raising an exception https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6329
### Added
- Added `DOM.query_one_optional`
- Added `default` parameter to `get_component_rich_style` get_component_rich_style
### Changed
- Added super+c (command on mac) alternative bindings for copy, for terminals that support it (Ghostty does)
- Allow `Sparkline` to be of any height, not just 1 https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6171
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v7.3.0, fixes include a triple-click exception on the command palette. Additions include DOM.query_one_optional and a default parameter for get_component_rich_style. Changes include super+c alternative bindings for copy on supported terminals and allowing Sparkline to be any height.

chunks.title: The extended cursor release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The extended cursor release
Tag: v7.5.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-30T13:48:56Z; created_at: 2026-01-30T13:45:09Z
Release notes:
The DataTable row cursor will now extend the full width of the datatable, which looks better.
The DataTable will now only emit a `*Selected` message if clicking a second time.
### [7.5.0] - 2026-01-30
- The DataTable row cursor will extend to the full width if there is excess space https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6345
- The DataTable will send a selected event on click, only if the cell / row / column is currently highlighted https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6345
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v7.5.0, the DataTable row cursor extends to full width when excess space exists, and the DataTable emits a selected event on click only if the cell/row/column is currently highlighted.

chunks.title: The BLANK to NULL release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The BLANK to NULL release
Tag: v8.0.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-02-16T17:14:09Z; created_at: 2026-02-16T17:10:52Z
Release notes:
The major version change is due to changing `Select.BLANK` to `Select.NULL`, to avoid an unfortunate name clash.
See below for the full changes...
## [8.0.0] - 2026-02-16
### Added
- Added `mode` argument to `push_screen` and `push_screen_wait` to enable pushing a screen to a non-active mode https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6362
- Added `App.mode_change_signal` and `App.screen_change_signal` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6362
- Added `Tabs.get_tab` https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6362
- Added Catppuccin Frappe and Macchiato themes https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6335
### Changed
- It is no longer a NOOP and warning to dismiss a non-active screen. The dismiss will still work, but the screen may not update if the current mode is not active. https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6362
- Added 50ms delay when switching screens to allow state to udpate and prevent janky flash of old content https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6362
- Breaking change: Changed `Select.BLANK` to `Select.NULL` to avoid clash with newer `Widget.BLANK` Classvar https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6374
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.0.0, a breaking change renames Select.BLANK to Select.NULL to avoid a name clash. Additions include a mode argument to push_screen and push_screen_wait, App.mode_change_signal and App.screen_change_signal, Tabs.get_tab, and Catppuccin Frappe and Macchiato themes. Changes include allowing dismiss of non-active screens, a 50ms delay when switching screens, and the BLANK to NULL rename.

chunks.title: The Juicy Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Juicy Release
Tag: v8.0.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-01T19:17:53Z; created_at: 2026-03-01T19:17:23Z
Release notes:
Small update
## [8.0.1] - 2026-03-01
### Fixed
- `DirectoryTree` runs more operations in a thread to avoid micro-freezes
### Changes
- Some tweaks to garbage collection to reduce gc time https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6402
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.0.1, DirectoryTree runs more operations in a thread to avoid micro-freezes, and garbage collection tweaks reduce GC time.

Release notes:

- Themes are now in alphabetical order in command palette https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6405
### Fixed

chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.0.2, themes are ordered alphabetically in the command palette, and issues with Directory Tree are fixed.

chunks.title: The Paused Released
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Paused Released
Tag: v8.1.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-10T03:07:00Z; created_at: 2026-03-10T02:30:46Z
Release notes:
This release should smooth scrolling large documents, particularly for Python < 3.14
## [8.1.0] - 2026-03-10
### Changed
- Replace circuar references in DOM with weak references to improve GC times https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6410
- When animating an attribute a second time, the original `on_complete` is now called https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6410
### Added
- Added experimental `App.PAUSE_GC_ON_SCROLL_` boolean (disabled by default) https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6410
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.1.0, circular references in the DOM are replaced with weak references to improve GC times, the original on_complete is called when animating an attribute a second time, and an experimental App.PAUSE_GC_ON_SCROLL_ boolean is added (disabled by default).

chunks.title: The Select Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The Select Release
Tag: v8.2.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-27T09:30:19Z; created_at: 2026-03-27T09:27:06Z
Release notes:
This release enhances text selection, with auto-scrolling, and the ability to select across container widgets.
This work was sponsored by Mistral AI.
## [8.2.0] - 2026-03-27
### Added
- Auto-scrolling on select https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6440
- Selecting over containers https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6440
- Added `App.ENABLE_SELECT_AUTO_SCROLL`, `App.SELECT_AUTO_SCROLL_LINES`, `App.SELECT_AUTO_SCROLL_SPEED` to tweak auto scrolling behavior https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6440
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.2.0, text selection is enhanced with auto-scrolling and the ability to select across container widgets. App.ENABLE_SELECT_AUTO_SCROLL, App.SELECT_AUTO_SCROLL_LINES, and App.SELECT_AUTO_SCROLL_SPEED are added to tweak auto-scrolling behavior. This work was sponsored by Mistral AI.

chunks.title: The Antsy Release
chunks.raw_text: Repository: Textualize/textual Release: The Antsy Release Tag: v8.2.5; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-04-30T08:06:47Z; created_at: 2026-04-30T08:01:39Z Release notes: This release adds support for dedicated ansi themes, which are also exposed from the command palette. There were a few tweaks to the theming system which may result in broken snapshots, but there should be no visual changes. ## [8.2.5] - 2026-04-30 ### Added - Two new themes: "ansi-dark" and "ansi-light" https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6513 - Added an `ansi` value to themes https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6513 ### Changed - `App.ansi_color` may now be `None` to use the `ansi` value from the theme. https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6513
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.2.5, dedicated ansi themes are added: ansi-dark and ansi-light. An ansi value is added to themes, and App.ansi_color may now be None to use the theme's ansi value. Theming system tweaks may break snapshots without visual changes.

chunks.title: The more selective release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The more selective release
Tag: v8.2.6; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-13T09:58:38Z; created_at: 2026-05-13T09:55:05Z
Release notes:
Improved text select logic. You can select text without first clicking on a content area.
May break snapshots without affecting appearance.
## [8.2.6] - 2026-04-13
### Fixed
- Fixed selection to the right of code fence blocks (may break some snapshots)
- Fixed Markdown code fences losing content when switching themes https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6537
### Added
- Added ability to select outside of text, so the user doesn't need to be quite so precise https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6523
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.2.6, text selection logic is improved, allowing selection outside of text for less precision. Fixes address selection to the right of code fence blocks and Markdown code fences losing content when switching themes.

chunks.title: The more Kitty Release
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: Textualize/textual
Release: The more Kitty Release
Tag: v8.2.7; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-05-19T10:57:19Z; created_at: 2026-05-19T10:48:41Z
Release notes:
This release adds additional support for the Kitty key protocol. Which enables some additional keys on some terminals. Additionally, Textual will report modifier keys as separate key events.
Additionally there are a few more shortcuts to the Text Area.
This release sponsored by Mistral AI. See release notes for detail.
## [8.2.7] - 2026-05-19
### Added
- Added support for Kitty key protocol "Report all keys as escape codes" which enabled alt+backspace on Warp https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6544
- Added support for detecting separate modifier keys for terminals that support the Kitty key protocol https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6544
- Added `TEXTUAL_DISABLE_KITTY_KEY` env var to disable Kitty key protocol support (debug aid). https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6544
### Changed
- Undo/redo/copy/cut/paste in TextArea will now work with cmd+ on supported terminals https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6543
- In TextArea, ctrl+u will now delete a newline if the cursor is at the start https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6543
- in TextArea alt+delete is now bound to delete word right https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6543
- Text opacity applied to an ansi theme will now set the dim attribute if the opacity is < 50% https://github.com/Textualize/textual/pull/6546
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the Textualize/textual release v8.2.7, additional Kitty key protocol support is added, enabling alt+backspace on Warp and detection of separate modifier keys. A TEXTUAL_DISABLE_KITTY_KEY env var is added. TextArea shortcuts are updated: undo/redo/copy/cut/paste work with cmd+, ctrl+u deletes newline at start, alt+delete deletes word right. Text opacity on ansi themes sets dim if opacity < 50%. Sponsored by Mistral AI.

questions.query_text: In the Textualize/textual releases, which release adds the `pointer` rule to TCSS for customizing mouse pointer appearance over widgets?

answer_support.answer_text: Adds a `pointer` rule to TCSS, so you can change how the mouse pointer looks over a given widget.

questions.query_text: Across the Textualize/textual releases, how does the performance improvement in v6.12.0 differ from the performance improvement in v7.0.1?

options.text: v6.12.0 fixes unnecessary style updates when popping screens, while v7.0.1 adds a refresh_styles boolean to ScreenResult to further reduce style updates during screen transitions.
v6.12.0 adds Widget.BLANK for rendering optimization, while v7.0.1 removes superfluous style updates when setting display.
v6.12.0 reduces GC time, while v7.0.1 fixes a performance issue with large scrollable containers.
v6.12.0 speeds up transitions by adding a 50ms delay, while v7.0.1 replaces circular references with weak references.

questions.expected_answer: v6.12.0 fixes unnecessary style updates when popping screens, while v7.0.1 adds a refresh_styles boolean to ScreenResult to further reduce style updates during screen transitions.

answer_support.answer_text: Fixed unnecessary style update when popping screens, which may have caused noticable pauses changing screens (with a lot of widgets)
Added a `refresh_styles` boolean to the `ScreenResult` message which reduces style updates when popping screens

questions.query_text: In the Textualize/textual release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds the `TextSelected` event, (2) a release that adds the `pointer` rule to TCSS, (3) a release that enhances text selection with auto-scrolling and selecting across containers, (4) a release that adds dedicated ansi themes ansi-dark and ansi-light.

answer_support.answer_text: Auto-scrolling on select ... Selecting over containers
